reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. Gate 1 (Dead Code): Function VerifyCellKZGProofBatchFromBlobData is called from production code at handlers.go:1090 for Fulu block validation. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): Issue is in client's own validation logic, not dependent on trusted data source corruption. Gate 3 (Code Verification): Code reading is factually correct - validation.go:135 calls ComputeCells, then validation.go:149 accesses cells[columnIndex] in loop 0..127 without validating len(cells)==128. Gate 4 (Exploitability): Passes under exception clause - while the bug requires c-kzg-4844 library defect (code-intrinsic), a panic in cell processing causes validator DoS and potential consensus impact, qualifying as protocol violation. Defense-in-depth validation is missing. 01e property PROP-57888860-post-003 from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json requires 'compute_cells must produce exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells from a valid blob'. Severity justified as Medium per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json criteria: single-client DoS affecting Prysm's ~31% network share, within 5-33% threshold for Medium severity.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: compute_cells must produce exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells from a valid blob. (forall compute_cells(blob) -> cells: len(cells) == CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB)
